title: pinguicula vulgaris subsp. longifolia
intro: Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. longifolia, also known as the long-leaved butterwort, is a carnivorous plant species within the Pinguicula genus, characterized by its unique ability to obtain essential nutrients by capturing and digesting insects. Traditionally, Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. longifolia has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and aiding in wound healing. Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. longifolia is often prepared as a tincture or infusion for its medicinal uses, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
